claim: UN-Habitat estimates 1 billion people in informal settlements, projected to 3 billion by 2050, with settlement residents having 1.5-3x child mortality and 2-5x infectious disease burden.
method: Reviewed World Cities Report chapters on informal settlements, health access, and projections.
limitations: Settlement population estimates are modeled, not directly counted.
Health-access estimates reflect available data which undersamples informal settlements.

claim: IDEAMAPS project has produced machine-learning-derived informal settlement boundary maps at 10m resolution for major LMIC cities, with validation accuracy of 75-85 percent against expert-drawn boundaries.
method: Reviewed IDEAMAPS methodology, validation results, and city coverage.
limitations: ML classification accuracy varies by city and neighborhood typology.
Settlement boundaries change rapidly and maps may be outdated.
